labelSource: model gateway (reasoning tier), deterministic fallback per-finding

findings.why: icon-only control (glyph "☰") with no accessible name
findings.patch:
Element signature (from a11y snapshot): - generic "☰" [ref=e4] clickable [cursor:pointer, onclick]
Suggested fix — add an accessible name:
    - <el …>☰</el>
    + <el … aria-label="Open menu">☰</el>
(If the element renders a visible label elsewhere, prefer associating it via aria-labelledby.)

prDescription:
**chore(a11y): add missing aria-labels to 1 interactive element(s) on fundflow**

Automated accessibility remediation generated by the QA Evergreen Tier-4 a11y_remediator.
Each element below is interactive but has no accessible name, so assistive-technology users
cannot address it (and the locator strategy had to degrade or skip it).

1. Sign in as the analyst: generic "☰" → add `aria-label="Open menu"`

_Suggested labels were phrased by the model gateway (reasoning tier) where available, with a
deterministic fallback otherwise. Please review wording before merging._
